sections.paragraph:
Δερβαῖος, α, ον from Derbe ὁ Δ.. (Stephan. Byz. s.v. Ἄβαι) of Gaius Ac 20:4. The difficulty caused by the fact that a certain Gaius is called a Macedonian 19:29 is prob. the reason for the v.l. Δουβ[ή]ριος in D (of a Maced. city); K and SLake, JBL 53, ’34, 44f; s. Haenchen (Ger. 65f; Eng. tr. 52f).

Byz. Byz. = Byzantine, esp. in ref. to papyri

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

prob. prob. = probable, probably

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

JBL JBL = Journal of Biblical Literature—List 6

Haenchen Haenchen = EH., Commentary on Acts—List 6

Ger. Ger. = German

Eng. Eng. tr. = English translation

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 218
